title: Strengthening Food Industry Monitoring through Real-Time PCR Screening Programs
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Foodborne     illnesses     remain     a     major     public     health     challenge  ,   leading     to     significant     economic     and     health     burdens     worldwide  .   Strengthening     pathogen     detection     systems   in   the     food     industry     is     crucial     to     minimize     outbreaks     and     ensure     consumer     safety  . Real-  time   PCR (  qPCR  )   technology     offers     rapid  ,   sensitive  ,   and     specific     detection     of     a     wide     range     of     foodborne     pathogens  ,   including     bacteria  ,   viruses  ,   and     parasites  .   Implementing     qPCR-based     screening     programs     across     the     food     supply     chain     can     drastically     reduce     response     times  ,   prevent     large-scale     recalls  ,   and     build     consumer     trust  .   This     policy     brief     highlights     the     strategic     importance     of   real-  time   PCR   integration     into     food     safety     monitoring     systems  ,   emphasizing     infrastructure     development  ,   workforce     training  ,   and     regulatory     alignment     to     create     a     robust  ,   science-driven     food     surveillance     network     that     supports     national     food     security     and     public     health     resilience  .
